source_name: Code4rena Angle Findings and Analysis Report (2023-06)
finding_title: User may get less tokens than expected when collateral list order changes
summary: The order of ts.collateralList is not stable: Whenever LibSetters.revokeCollateral is used to revoke a collateral, it may change because of the swap that is performed. However, the function Redeemer.redeem relies on this order, as the user has to provide the minAmountsOut in the order of ts.collateralList. This can lead to situations where the user has crafted the minAmountsOut array when the order was still different, leading to unintended results (and potentially redemptions that the user did not want to accept). It also means that revoking a collateral can be challenging for the team / governance because it should never be done when a user has already prepared a redemption (either via the frontend which he had open or some other way to interact with the contract). But there is of course no way to know this.
root_cause: redemption output depends on mutable collateral list ordering, so collateral revocation can make users receive a different token mix than expected
broken_invariants: the collateral management path must not allow the condition described by "User may get less tokens than expected when collateral list order changes" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
users must retain a reliable exit path unless an explicitly designed and validated pause or restriction is active
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: The order of ts.collateralList is not stable: Whenever LibSetters.revokeCollateral is used to revoke a collateral, it may change because of the swap that is performed. However, the function Redeemer.redeem relies on this order, as the user has to provide the minAmountsOut in the order of ts.collateralList. This can lead to situations where the user has crafted the minAmountsOut array when the order was still different, leading to unintended results (and potentially redemptions that the user did not want to accept). It also means that revoking a collateral can be challenging for the team / gove

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; exploitability, affected value, or preconditions are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: The problem could be alleviated a bit by checking the length of minAmountsOut (making sure it is not longer than ts.collateralList). However, that would not help if a collateral is revoked and a new one is added. Another solution would be to provide pairs of token addresses and amounts, which would solve the problem completely.
